title: nolana humifusa subsp. spathulata
intro: Nolana humifusa subsp. spathulata, commonly known as a type of flowering plant in the morning glory family, is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Nolana humifusa subsp. spathulata include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and potentially alleviating symptoms of certain respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems are used in herbal preparations for their medicinal properties.
